what_it_does: Records a raised noticeboard signal that the operator has decided the global visual-fidelity decision with the chosen option 'Push the drawn style further first', and states that work gated on decision://global/visual-fidelity can resume.
observations: YAML frontmatter carries id, board:// address, type=signal, source=claude_code, state=raised, author_session=operator, and a single attached_to link to decision://global/visual-fidelity.
History shows one transition from null to raised by operator at the same timestamp.
Body text names the decision, the chosen option, and the resume gate in plain language.
No code symbols are present in the file.
standouts: Decision signal for global visual-fidelity is now raised.
Chosen option is explicitly 'Push the drawn style further first'.
Gated work on decision://global/visual-fidelity is authorized to resume.
conventions: Markdown file with YAML frontmatter metadata.
Uses URI schemes board:// and decision:// for addressing and linking.
Follows a signal/state-machine pattern with a history transition log.
Uses a links list with kind attached_to for cross-referencing.
concerns: channel and thread are empty strings, which may limit routing context for the signal.
created and updated timestamps are identical, so no subsequent edit history is captured.
notes: A raised noticeboard signal that the visual-fidelity decision is settled on pushing the drawn style further first, telling gated work it may resume.
questions: Which lane or operator is responsible for performing the actual resume step?
What specific work items are gated on decision://global/visual-fidelity?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches content: it records a decided signal and the resume gate for dependent work.
intention_signals: To communicate a resolved decision option to downstream participants and authorize resumption of work that was waiting on that decision.
novelty: Uses a lightweight noticeboard item as a durable, URI-addressable decision signal with explicit gating semantics.
contribution: Provides a timestamped, addressable record that a global decision has been decided with a specific option, giving downstream systems a clear point at which to resume gated work.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard signal item recording that the operator decided the global visual-fidelity decision by choosing 'Push the drawn style further first', raising the signal and indicating that gated work may resume.
intention_for_embedding: To broadcast the chosen visual-fidelity option and unblock work gated on decision://global/visual-fidelity.
